tldr: These tools solve different problems — Devenv reproduces dev environments, Melos manages Dart/Flutter monorepo packages — so this is a reframing, not a true migration, and only makes sense if the project is Flutter-centric.
why_switch: Project is Dart/Flutter and needs versioning + changelog automation
Coordinated publishing of Flutter plugins to pub.dev
Run scripts across multiple Dart packages with filtering
Idiomatic Dart/Flutter tooling instead of generic Nix shell
Drop Nix dependency if reproducible env was the only reason to keep Devenv
what_to_export: List of Dart/Flutter SDK versions pinned in devenv.nix
Workspace package layout (paths to each pubspec.yaml)
Scripts defined in devenv.nix you want to keep
CI commands that activated the Devenv shell
Pre-commit hooks relevant to Dart code
export_method: There is no automated path. Inventory devenv.nix entries by hand. For Dart/Flutter SDK pinning, decide whether to keep Nix/Devenv solely for SDK install or switch to fvm / asdf / Hermit / official Flutter installer. Melos itself does not manage SDK versions — confirm current behavior in the official Melos docs.
import_method: Install Melos following the official guide (typically dart pub global activate melos). Add a melos.yaml at the repo root listing your packages and scripts. Run melos bootstrap to link local packages. Move cross-package commands previously in devenv.nix scripts into melos.yaml scripts. Keep your SDK manager (fvm/asdf/Hermit) separate. Verify current syntax and capabilities against official Melos documentation.

concept_mapping.note: Cross-package scripts move here; one-off shell scripts stay as scripts
Use fvm, asdf, or official installer; Melos doesn't pin the SDK
Melos only manages packages; reproducibility is on you
Run separately via Docker Compose or similar

gotchas: Melos is Dart/Flutter only — non-Dart tooling needs another home
Melos does not replace Devenv's reproducibility — pair it with an SDK manager
Service orchestration has no Melos equivalent
Versioning behavior depends on Melos config; review release flow before flipping CI
If team uses non-Flutter languages too, you may end up running both tools
Validate exact melos.yaml schema against current official docs

email.subject: Proposal: adopt Melos for our Flutter monorepo workflow
email.body:
Hi team,

Devenv reproduces our toolchain, but for our Flutter monorepo we still lack first-class versioning, changelogs, and coordinated pub.dev publishing. Melos is purpose-built for this.

The two tools aren't substitutes — Melos handles package orchestration, Devenv handles environment reproduction. Plan: keep an SDK manager for Flutter pinning, drop Devenv if we don't need its services, adopt Melos for workspace scripts and releases. Estimated 3-8 hours.

Thoughts?

Thanks
